g.text: L1 Behavior | Partial hits, some waste | All hits after warmup
Branch Predictor | Trivial (no branches) | Trivial (no branches)

div.p: Cache Line 0 (64 bytes) [px][py][pz][vx][vy][vz][ma][pad][px][py]... └─ particle 0 ────────┘ └─ p1 ─ Used: 24 bytes (x,y,z,vx,vy,vz) Wasted: 40 bytes (62.5%)
Cache Line (x array) (64 bytes) [x0][x1][x2][x3][x4][x5][x6][x7][x8]... Used: 64 bytes (16 floats) Wasted: 0 bytes (100%) ×6 arrays: x, y, z, vx, vy, vz

g.text: Data Layout | Interleaved xyz | Separate arrays

div.p: i=0: Load CL0 [p0.x,y,z,vx,vy,vz,mass,p1.x,y...] ↓ Access p0.x, p0.vx (12 bytes used, 52 wasted) i=1: Load CL0 (HIT) - p1 already loaded ↓ Access p1.x, p1.vx i=2: Load CL1 [p2.x,y,z,vx,vy,vz,mass,p3.x,y...] ↓ NEW CACHE LINE NEEDED Result: ~n/2 cache misses, poor utilization

i=0-15: Load x[0:15] → 1 cache line (64 bytes) Load vx[0:15] → 1 cache line Process 16 particles! i=16-31: Load x[16:31] → 1 cache line (prefetched!) Load vx[16:31] → 1 cache line (prefetched!) Result: 6n/16 = 0.375n cache lines, 100% utilization

ul.li: Both loops compute position += velocity × dt
Same number of FLOPs (6n multiply-adds) But cache behavior differs by 8x+ SoA transforms a memory-bound loop into a compute-bound loop. The CPU spends time doing math instead of waiting for RAM.

g.text: vfmadd132ps ymm0, ymm1, ymm2 ; x += vx*dt (x8)
